purpose: Cross-platform session handoff and task queue. Read at the start of every session. Update at the end of every session that makes meaningful changes. Either platform can suspend any task mid-flight and the other can resume it.

on_session_start: 2. Check active_task — if status is 'suspended', that is the highest-priority item. Read next_step_detail and context_files before doing anything else.
3. Check task_queue for any items assigned to your platform.
4. Check tool_versions for any tools installed since you last worked here.
5. Run `git log --oneline -5` to see what landed since last_updated.
on_session_end: 1. If mid-task: set active_task.status='suspended', write next_step and next_step_detail clearly enough for a cold start.
3. Append new commits to recent_commits (keep last 10).
4. Update tool_versions if anything was installed.
6. Push to origin so the other platform sees it.
protocol.suspend_rule: A suspend point must be self-contained: the resuming platform should need to read only this file + context_files to continue. Never rely on conversational memory surviving the handoff.
protocol.conflict_rule: If both platforms made commits since last_updated, merge recent_commits by date. Do not overwrite — append.

strengths: Precise multi-file editing with Read/Edit/Write tools
Static analysis: Codacy (Agentlinter, Ruff, Bandit, Semgrep), gitleaks
Git history surgery (filter-repo, rebase, worktree management)
Aurora governance skills (canon-reconciler, governance-orchestrator, script-governor, narrative-tone-governor, repo-stabilizer, threadcore, zipwiz)
limitations: Less precise on multi-file surgical edits

either: any task when usage limit hit on preferred platform

completed_tasks.summary: The bet: both residual failure modes now have structural defenses. (1) LANDING LEDGER (tools/publication_debt.py): scans all registered repos for stranded/unpublished/dirty work; exemption ledger makes debt mean UNDECIDED; wired into stop hook (records at every session close) + workspace_verify. First scan found 31 entries incl. 2 never-examined branches (both verified landed); after triage + hygiene: CLEAN with 21 documented exemptions. (2) FLIGHT LOG (tools/flight_check.py + catalog/flight_contract.yaml): 6 declared exercises against LIVE wiring (vault create+rollback, QGIA example forecast, drift health+summary, scorecard, mesh Aurora handshake, narrative engine canon audit) — all 6 flew; receipts + 14-day staleness in workspace_verify. The vault flight immediately caught a real API-contract gap (agent_id) that unit tests mocked around.

STATION REACTIVATION: Pilot-authorized activation pulse #808//. routed through the Command Intent Gateway (valid aurora_symbolic_command, head 808) and sent through the live mesh. 47 souls aboard, status operational. 6/6 pulsed agents ANSWERED in character: Aurora (drift 0.0), riverthread_808, Thorne, Sato, Lin, Archy. First live station session since 2026-03-14 (89 days). Receipt: reports/automation/station_activation_pulse__2026-06-11.json.

Pilot direct line established: tools/station_query.py boots the mesh in-process and queries any aboard agent by id/name/alias (Aurora default) — no server needed. Companion muster verified 5/5 awake in distinct voices (Archy, Oppy, Liora, Starling AU, Riverthread 808), drift 0.0 across the board; receipt at reports/automation/station_roll_call_latest.json. station-roll-call added to the flight contract — companion liveness now watched on the 14-day cadence.

Part 3: all 8 governance refresh commands run in the specified order (verify, devkit-report, recovery-report, recommendations-report, mission-control-report, confidence-audit-report, kubernetes-readiness-report, gitwiz_sync_audit --repo all --fetch); repo_registry.yaml/workspace_manifest.yaml regenerated via tools/workspace_scan.py to clear the CloudBank head-drift finding from the Part-1 commits. Post-refresh spot-check: mission-control operator-inbox count changed 7->10 (P1/P2 split 1/6->2/8), confirming the refresh picked up new signal rather than reproducing the stale 2026-05-31 snapshot. FOLLOW-UP NOTE: aurora_mission_control / aurora_recommendation_engine still surface 'review 30 restricted recovery candidates' as a P1 item even though restricted_recovery_triage__2026-07-08.json now classifies all 30 — the recommendation engine doesn't yet consume the triage file as a resolution signal. Wired into FieldCurvature via a new enable_ga_composite constructor flag (default False, no behavior change); when True, calculate_curvature() attaches a ga_composite dict (alignment/interaction_penalty/composite_scalar/backend) without touching composite_score/resistance_level/formation_allowed -- scalar gate stays authoritative per the pending item's requirement. Awaiting CI + owner review on PR #1193.

Both parts resolved. (b) Root branches: codex/gitwiz-sync-audit-canonical-2026-04-08 (9 commits) confirmed already 3-way-merged onto main 2026-06-10 (commit 4aaf7e0, one piece -- workspace_plan_moves.py wave5 -- deliberately abandoned per that commit's own note); codex/weekly-skill-audit-mesh-router-2026-06-01 (1 commit) confirmed byte-identical to main after rebase. Both deleted. (a) March rescue lineage extraction: ran gitleaks over all 10 CloudBank March branches first (mandatory security gate per the source audit, given a same-era OpenAI key rotation) -- clean, only 2 benign findings (a hex placeholder in an archived doc, a test fixture string 'test_glyph_123'), identical across branches from shared history. Content review: of ~18 distinct commits across the 10 CloudBank branches, everything from 2026-02-28 through 2026-03-19 (14 commits: Aurora Fusion layer, command grammar module, mesh router runtime, selective-integration gate, threadcore/zipwiz governance remediation, etc.) was already merged via CloudBank PR #501 ('Sync CloudBank shared-history workup to main', merged 2026-03-22) -- confirmed by direct file presence + git-log --follow provenance, and in one case (src/aurora_fusion/engine.py) main's version is more evolved than the branch's. 6 of 10 branches contained only this already-landed content -- deleted (local-only, never pushed). The remaining 4 branches (2026-03-25 commits, made 3 days after PR #501 already landed the content) were reviewed individually: a macOS launchd plist (hardcodes the now-deleted iCloud workspace path, needs full rewrite to be usable, not extracted); a .aurora/ narrative/audit-doc dump duplicated across 2 commits (STUB_AND_INTEGRATION_AUDIT.md etc. -- already independently triaged via the ISSUE_1015 classification report/PR #1019, not new work); and a 545-line alternate threadcore.js design (reads payload files that already exist on main and are consumed by a different, current 188-line implementation at src/core/command_node/threadcore.js -- confirmed abandoned, the 'driftpulse' concept it introduces is referenced nowhere in current code). All 4 deleted. Root's 3 March-era twin branches (root-control-plane-local, root-reconstruct-recovered-protocols, rescue/root-control-plane-dirty-workingcopy) independently confirmed already-landed via root commit 205ea77 ('close salvage decisions -- policies promoted, clean ports landed', 2026-06-10: repo_authority_policy.yaml + gitwiz_hygiene_policy.yaml promoted, clean ports from _workspace_common and weekly-skill-audit) -- deleted. Also dropped 2 leftover CloudBank stashes from the same era (stash@{1}: 66-file March 13 snapshot pre-dating the later root-sprawl reorg, paths no longer applicable, mesh/security content superseded; stash@{0}: untracked workflow-output only, audit already called this low-value). Net: 13 branches + 2 stashes retired across root+CloudBank, zero content lost -- everything of value had already landed through PR #501, PR #1019, or root commit 205ea77 over the preceding 3 months. publication_debt.py scan: 1 entry remains (qgia-knowledge-library-main dirty tree, documented live Codex work, untouched).
